## Library Archives

Princeton University Library archival collections

1. Format: Collection


### [Gordon family correspondence, circa 1870-1902](https://findingaids.princeton.edu/catalog/AC482)


   - Description:


     The collection consists of letters written and received by members of the Gordon family of Fannettsburg, Pennsylvania between the 1870s and approximately 1905. Some of the letters cover the period when Clarence Gordon '1891, son of Jeremiah Smith Gordon '1853, was a student at Princeton, as well as when Mary Gordon and Clementine Gordon attended Wilson College in Chambersburg, Pennsylvania.



     The collection consists of letters written and received by members of the Gordon family of Fannettsburg, Pennsylvania between the 1870s and…

     Show abstract
2. Format: Collection


### [Grover Cleveland Collection, 1879-1984](https://findingaids.princeton.edu/catalog/AC348)


   - Description:


     Grover Cleveland was the twenty-second (1885-1889) and twenty-fourth (1893-1897) president of the United States. After leaving the White House he retired to Princeton, N.J where he was a Trustee of Princeton University. The Grover Cleveland Papers consist of collected research materials regarding Grover Cleveland, his life in Princeton, and his relation to the University.



     Grover Cleveland was the twenty-second (1885-1889) and twenty-fourth (1893-1897) president of the United States. After leaving the White House he…

     Show abstract
3. Format: Collection


### [Pennington Satterthwaite Papers, 1882-1893](https://findingaids.princeton.edu/catalog/AC355)


   - Description:


     Pennington Satterthwaite was a graduate of Princeton University's Class of 1893 and an architect. The Pennington Satterthwaite Papers consists of various documents pertaining to Satterthwaite's early life up to and including his time as an undergraduate at Princeton University (then the College of New Jersey).
